content.text: Make simple CRUD requests with NestJS

content.html: This article details the steps involved in creating a basic CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) API using NestJS, a popular framework for building Node.js applications.

items: Node.js and npm (or yarn) installed on your system.

content.html: NestJS utilizes a modular approach, where functionalities are organized into modules. The core building blocks include:

items: Modules: Group controllers, services, and other providers.
Controllers: Handle incoming requests and interact with services.
Services: Implement business logic and interact with data sources.
Data Transfer Objects (DTOs): Define DTOs to handle data validation and transfer between layers.
Filtering and Searching: Implement functionalities to filter and search tasks based on criteria.
Download and install Postman, a popular API client.
Use Postman to send requests (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to the defined API endpoints with appropriate data.
Verify the responses and ensure they align with expectations.

node -v # should print `v20.14.0`v20.14.0

content.text: Create a Nest Controller without a test

content.code: nest g controller tasks --no-spec
import { Controller } from '@nestjs/common';@Controller('tasks')export class TasksController {}

content.alt: Make simple CRUD requests with NestJS

content.code: nest g service tasks --no-spec

import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';import { TasksController } from './tasks.controller';import { TasksService } from './tasks.service';@Module({  controllers: [TasksController],  providers: [TasksService],})export class TasksModule {}

import { Controller } from '@nestjs/common';import { TasksService } from './tasks.service';@Controller('tasks')export class TasksController {  tasksService: TasksService;  constructor(tasksService: TasksService) {    this.tasksService = tasksService;  }  helloWorld() {    this.tasksService.helloWorld();  }}

content.text: Set service as private element in Controller

content.code: import { Controller } from '@nestjs/common';import { TasksService } from './tasks.service';@Controller('tasks')export class TasksController {  constructor(private tasksService: TasksService) {}  helloWorld() {    this.tasksService.helloWorld();  }}

import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';import { TasksService } from './tasks.service';@Controller('tasks')export class TasksController {  constructor(private tasksService: TasksService) {}  @Get()  getAllTasks() {    return this.tasksService.getAllTasks();  }}
@Injectable()export class TasksService {  private tasks = [];  getAllTasks() {    this.tasks.push({ id: 1, title: 'Task 1', description: 'Description 1' });    return this.tasks;  }}

content.code: export interface Task {  id: number;  title: string;  description: string;  status: TaskStatus;}export enum TaskStatus {  OPEN = 'OPEN',  IN_PROGRESS = 'IN_PROGRESS',  DONE = 'DONE',}

import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';import { Task } from './tasks.model';@Injectable()export class TasksService {  private tasks: Task[] = [];  getAllTasks(): Task[] {    return this.tasks;  }}

import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';import { TasksService } from './tasks.service';import { Task } from './tasks.model';@Controller('tasks')export class TasksController {  constructor(private tasksService: TasksService) {}  @Get()  getAllTasks(): Task[] {    return this.tasksService.getAllTasks();  }}
yarn add uuidimport { v4 as uuid } from 'uuid';id: uuid(),

createTask(title: string, description: string): Task {    const task: Task = {      id: uuid(),      title,      description,      status: TaskStatus.OPEN,    };    this.tasks.push(task);    return task;  }

@Post()  createTask(    @Body('title') title: string,    @Body('description') description: string,  ): Task {    console.log(title, description);    return this.tasksService.createTask(title, description);  }

content.html: http request -> Controller -> Service -> Controller -> http response

content.code: export class CreateTaskDto {  title: string;  description: string;}
@Post()  createTask(@Body() createTaskDto: CreateTaskDto): Task {    console.log(createTaskDto.title, createTaskDto.description);    return this.tasksService.createTask(createTaskDto);  }

createTask(createTaskDto: CreateTaskDto): Task {    const { title, description } = createTaskDto;    const task: Task = {      id: uuid(),      title,      description,      status: TaskStatus.OPEN,    };    this.tasks.push(task);    return task;  }
@Get('/:id')  getTaskById(@Param('id') id: number): Task {    return this.tasksService.getTaskById(id);  }
getTaskById(id: number): Task {    return this.tasks.find((task) => task.id === id);  }
@Delete('/:id')  deleteTask(@Param('id') id: number): void {    return this.tasksService.deleteTask(id);  }
deleteTask(id: number): void {    this.tasks = this.tasks.filter((task) => task.id !== id);  }

content.alt: Make simple CRUD requests with NestJS

content.code: @Patch('/:id/status')  updateTaskStatus(    @Param('id') id: string,    @Body('status') status: Task['status'],  ): Task {    return this.tasksService.updateTaskStatus(id, status);  }
updateTaskStatus(id: string, status: TaskStatus): Task {    const task = this.getTaskById(id);    console.log(status);    task.status = status;    return task;  }
import { TaskStatus } from '../tasks.model';export class GetTasksFilterDto {  status?: TaskStatus;  search?: string;}

@Get()  getTasks(@Query() filterDTO: GetTasksFilterDto): Task[] {    if (Object.keys(filterDTO).length) {      return this.tasksService.getTasksWithFilters(filterDTO);    } else {      return this.tasksService.getAllTasks();    }  }

getTasksWithFilters(filterDTO: GetTasksFilterDto): Task[] {    const { status, search } = filterDTO;    let tasks = this.getAllTasks();    if (status) {      tasks = tasks.filter((task) => task.status === status);    }    if (search) {      tasks = tasks.filter(        (task) =>          task.title.includes(search) || task.description.includes(search),      );    }    return tasks;  }
